content.text: Microsoft Entra Connect (formerly Azure AD Connect) is the on-premises software that bridges Active Directory Domain Services and Microsoft Entra ID. It synchronizes user, group, device, and contact objects from AD to Entra ID on a configurable schedule (default 30 minutes), enabling hybrid identity — users have a single identity usable both on-premises and in the cloud.

The sync engine has three layers: an AD connector (reads from AD via LDAP), a metaverse (a canonical in-memory representation of each object), and an Entra ID connector (writes to Entra via the Graph synchronization API). Attribute flow rules and scoping filters determine which objects and attributes are projected and how values are transformed in transit.

attrs.alt: Entra Connect Sync architecture: AD DS, sync engine with metaverse, Entra ID, password hash sync, writeback

content.text: Entra Connect must be installed on a Windows Server 2016 or later that has line-of-sight to at least one AD domain controller. The server should not be a domain controller itself. You need a hybrid identity administrator (or global admin) account in Entra ID and an AD account with Enterprise Admin privileges for the initial configuration.

# Pre-flight checks before installing Entra Connect

# 1. Verify OS version (must be Windows Server 2016+)
[System.Environment]::OSVersion.Version

# 2. Check .NET Framework version (4.7.2+ required)
(Get-ItemProperty 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\NET Framework Setup\NDP\v4\Full').Release
# 461808 = 4.7.2, 528040 = 4.8

# 3. Check TLS 1.2 is enabled (required for Entra communication)
[Net.ServicePointManager]::SecurityProtocol
# Should include Tls12

# 4. Verify DNS resolution for Entra ID endpoints
Resolve-DnsName 'login.microsoftonline.com'
Resolve-DnsName 'adminwebservice.microsoftonline.com'

# 5. Test outbound connectivity
Test-NetConnection -ComputerName 'login.microsoftonline.com' -Port 443
Test-NetConnection -ComputerName 'provisioningapi.microsoftonline.com' -Port 443

# 6. Verify AD service account permissions
# The AD connector account needs:
#   - Replicate Directory Changes
#   - Replicate Directory Changes All
#   (on the domain NC, for password hash sync)
#   - Read permissions on all synced OUs
Import-Module ActiveDirectory
(Get-ADUser 'ADConnectorAccount' -Properties MemberOf).MemberOf
LocalDB (auto-installed) for < 100k objects; SQL Server 2012+ for larger
4 GB minimum; 8 GB recommended for environments > 50k objects
Enterprise Admin for initial install; dedicated service account for ongoing sync

By default, Entra Connect syncs all objects in all domains. Scoping filters restrict synchronization to specific OUs, domains, or object attributes. OU filtering is the most common approach — it lets you sync only the OUs containing users, groups, and devices that need cloud access, excluding service accounts and administrative objects.

Scoping filters are evaluated in the connector space before projection to the metaverse. An object excluded by a scoping filter is not synchronized and will be deleted from Entra ID if it was previously synced. Test filter changes in a staging environment first.

Entra Connect supports three authentication architectures. Password Hash Synchronization (PHS) syncs a hash of the password hash to Entra ID, enabling cloud authentication without any on-premises infrastructure. Pass-Through Authentication (PTA) validates passwords against on-premises AD in real time using lightweight agents. Federation with AD FS offloads authentication entirely to on-premises infrastructure but requires the most operational overhead.
NTLM hash of hash synced to Entra every 2 min
Auth agent validates credentials against AD in real time
Entra redirects to AD FS; SAML token issued by AD FS
AD FS farm must be available

# Check current authentication method
Connect-MgGraph -Scopes 'Organization.Read.All'
(Get-MgOrganization).OnPremisesSyncEnabled

# For PHS: verify password hash sync is running
Import-Module ADSync
Get-ADSyncScheduler  # shows LastSyncCycleStartedDate, SyncCycleEnabled

# Check PHS-specific connector run status
Get-ADSyncConnectorRunStatus | Select-Object ConnectorName, Result, StartDate, EndDate

# For PTA: list authentication agents and their health
Connect-MgGraph -Scopes 'Directory.Read.All'
Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Uri 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/onPremisesPublishingProfiles/authentication/agents' |
    Select-Object -ExpandProperty value |
    Select-Object machineName, status, externalIp, appVersion

# Switch from PTA to PHS (staged rollout example)
# Use staged rollout to test PHS with a subset of users before full cutover
Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method POST `
    -Uri 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/directory/featureRolloutPolicies' `
    -Body (@{
        displayName    = 'PHS Staged Rollout'
        feature        = 'passwordHashSync'
        isEnabled      = $true
        isAppliedToOrganization = $false
    } | ConvertTo-Json)

PHS does not sync the plaintext password or even the NTLM hash directly. It syncs a salted hash of the MD4 hash (NTLM hash), computed using PBKDF2 with HMAC-SHA256 and 1000 iterations, then further salted with a random value specific to Entra ID. The result is a one-way transformation that Entra can use to validate passwords but that cannot be reversed to the original credential.

# Monitor password hash synchronization status
Import-Module ADSync

# Get last password sync statistics
Get-ADSyncCSObject -ConnectorName 'Entra ID Connector' |
    Where-Object { $_.PendingExport.AttributeChanges.Name -contains 'passwordProfile' } |
    Select-Object -First 5 | Select-Object AnchorValue, ConnectorName

# Check if a specific user's password was synced
$userDN = 'CN=Alice Smith,OU=Users,DC=contoso,DC=com'
$csObj = Get-ADSyncCSObject -ConnectorName 'contoso.com' -DistinguishedName $userDN
$csObj.Attributes | Where-Object Name -eq 'unicodePwd' |
    Select-Object Name, LastImportDateTime

# Force a password hash sync cycle (useful after bulk password resets)
Invoke-ADSyncRunProfile -ConnectorName 'contoso.com' -RunProfileName 'Delta Import'
Invoke-ADSyncRunProfile -ConnectorName 'contoso.com' -RunProfileName 'Delta Synchronization'
Invoke-ADSyncRunProfile -ConnectorName 'Entra ID Connector' -RunProfileName 'Export'

# Enable/disable password hash sync (requires restart of ADSync service)
Set-ADSyncPasswordSyncConfiguration -SourceConnector 'contoso.com' `
    -TargetConnector 'Entra ID Connector' -Enable $true

Synchronization rules define how attributes flow between the connector space and the metaverse, and between the metaverse and Entra ID. Default rules handle standard attributes (UPN, displayName, mail, etc.). Custom rules add transformations, map custom attributes, or override default precedence. Rules have a precedence number — lower numbers win when two rules set the same attribute.

# View all synchronization rules and their precedence
Get-ADSyncRule | Sort-Object Precedence |
    Select-Object Precedence, Name, Direction, ConnectorName, LinkType |
    Format-Table -AutoSize

# Inspect attribute flow for a specific rule
$rule = Get-ADSyncRule -Identifier '<rule-guid>'
$rule.AttributeFlowMappings | Select-Object Source, Destination, FlowType, ExecuteOnce

# Create a custom inbound rule: flow extensionAttribute1 from AD to Entra ID
# This adds a custom attribute to cloud users without modifying the default rules
$newRule = New-ADSyncRule `
    -Name 'In from AD - Custom Attribute flow' `
    -Identifier (New-Guid).ToString() `
    -Direction Inbound `
    -Precedence 50 `
    -LinkType Join `
    -ConnectorName 'contoso.com' `
    -MetaverseObjectType user `
    -EnablePasswordSync $false

Add-ADSyncAttributeFlowMapping `
    -SynchronizationRule $newRule `
    -Source 'extensionAttribute1' `
    -Destination 'extensionAttribute1' `
    -FlowType Direct `
    -ValueMergeType Update

Add-ADSyncRule $newRule

# After adding rules, start a full sync to apply
Start-ADSyncSyncCycle -PolicyType Initial

Entra Connect Health provides a cloud-based monitoring dashboard for sync errors, latency, and agent health. The on-premises sync log and the Synchronization Service Manager (miisclient.exe) provide detailed per-object diagnostic information. Common issues include attribute uniqueness violations (duplicate UPNs or proxy addresses), object filtering, and connector space conflicts.

# Query sync errors via Microsoft Graph (requires Entra Connect Health)
Connect-MgGraph -Scopes 'AuditLog.Read.All','Organization.Read.All'

# List current sync errors from Entra ID perspective
Get-MgOrganizationOnPremiseSynchronization | Select-Object -ExpandProperty State

# Get objects with sync errors
Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Uri 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/users?$filter=onPremisesProvisioningErrors/any()&$select=displayName,userPrincipalName,onPremisesProvisioningErrors' |
    Select-Object -ExpandProperty value |
    ForEach-Object {
        [PSCustomObject]@{
            UPN    = $_.userPrincipalName
            Name   = $_.displayName
            Errors = $_.onPremisesProvisioningErrors | ForEach-Object { "$($_.category): $($_.value)" }
        }
    }

# Diagnose a specific object in the connector space (on-premises)
Import-Module ADSync
$upn = 'bob@contoso.com'
$csObj = Get-ADSyncCSObject -ConnectorName 'contoso.com' `
    -Filter "userPrincipalName -eq '$upn'"
if ($csObj) {
    # Check export errors
    $csObj.ExportError | Select-Object ErrorCode, ErrorLiteral, ServerErrorDetail
    # Check pending changes
    $csObj.PendingExport.AttributeChanges | Select-Object Name, ValueChanges
} else {
    Write-Warning "Object not found in connector space — check OU filtering"
}

# Fix a UPN uniqueness conflict: user exists in cloud with same UPN
# First, identify the conflicting user
Get-MgUser -Filter "userPrincipalName eq 'bob@contoso.com'" |
    Select-Object Id, DisplayName, UserPrincipalName, OnPremisesSyncEnabled
Duplicate UPN or proxyAddress in the tenant
Rename UPN in AD or remove the cloud-only duplicate
Invalid UPN format or characters in attribute value
Correct the AD attribute value; use admx/PowerShell to sanitize
Object type changed (e.g. user to contact)
Delete the cloud object and let sync recreate it
Use hard-match via ImmutableId or resolve the duplicate
Fix the attribute value in AD; check for null or too-long strings

Writeback reverses the sync direction: changes made in Entra ID flow back to on-premises Active Directory. Three writeback features are commonly used: password writeback (SSPR changes passwords in AD), group writeback (Entra groups become AD distribution/security groups), and device writeback (Entra-registered devices appear in AD for Kerberos targeting).

# Enable password writeback on the Entra Connect server
Import-Module ADSync

# Password writeback requires the AD connector account to have:
#   Reset Password
#   Write lockoutTime
#   Write pwdLastSet
# on all user OUs in scope

# Enable writeback via Set-ADSyncConfiguration (Entra Connect 2.x)
Set-ADSyncAADPasswordResetConfiguration -Enable $true -Credential (Get-Credential)

# Verify writeback is active
Get-ADSyncAADPasswordResetConfiguration

# Enable group writeback (v2: universal security groups written to AD)
# Run from the Entra Connect server; requires AD schema extension
Initialize-ADSyncGroupWriteBack -ADConnectorAccountDN 'CN=ADConnector,OU=ServiceAccounts,DC=contoso,DC=com' `
    -GroupWriteBackDN 'OU=EntraGroups,DC=contoso,DC=com'

Set-ADSyncAADCompanyFeature -GroupWritebackV2 $true

# Trigger a sync cycle after enabling writeback
Start-ADSyncSyncCycle -PolicyType Delta

# Verify a cloud group was written back to AD
Get-ADGroup -Filter { Name -like 'EntraGroup*' } -SearchBase 'OU=EntraGroups,DC=contoso,DC=com'

Entra Connect supports a staging mode server — a second installation that imports and synchronizes data but does not export changes to Entra ID or write back to AD. It acts as a warm standby. If the active server fails, you promote the staging server by disabling staging mode, which immediately begins exporting the already-synchronized data without needing a full import.

# Check if current server is in staging mode
Import-Module ADSync
(Get-ADSyncScheduler).StagingModeEnabled

# Promote a staging server to active (when primary fails)
# Step 1: Confirm the staging server's data is current
Get-ADSyncScheduler | Select-Object LastSyncCycleStartedDate, StagingModeEnabled

# Step 2: Disable staging mode
Set-ADSyncScheduler -StagingModeEnabled $false

# Step 3: Trigger an export cycle to push pending changes
Start-ADSyncSyncCycle -PolicyType Delta

# Step 4: Verify exports completed without error
Get-ADSyncConnectorRunStatus | Select-Object ConnectorName, RunProfileName, Result, StartDate

# Put the old primary into staging mode once it recovers (to prevent split-brain)
# On the recovered primary server:
Set-ADSyncScheduler -StagingModeEnabled $true

# Verify only ONE server has StagingModeEnabled = false at any time
# Two active servers would cause duplicate exports and sync conflicts

Never have two Entra Connect servers in active mode simultaneously. If both export to the same Entra tenant, you get duplicate object errors and unpredictable attribute values. Always keep the standby in staging mode until failover is confirmed necessary.

Entra Connect releases updates roughly monthly. Microsoft announces end-of-support dates for older versions; running an unsupported version means you receive no security fixes. Auto-upgrade handles minor version bumps for most configurations. Major version upgrades or installations with custom configurations require a manual in-place upgrade or swing migration.

# Check current Entra Connect version
(Get-ItemProperty 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Azure AD Connect').Version

# Check if auto-upgrade is enabled
(Get-ADSyncAutoUpgrade).AutoUpgradeState
# Returns: Enabled, Disabled, Suspended, or NotSupported

# Enable auto-upgrade
Set-ADSyncAutoUpgrade -AutoUpgradeState Enabled

# Manual upgrade: download latest MSI from Microsoft Download Center
# Run the installer on the active Entra Connect server
# The installer detects the existing installation and performs in-place upgrade

# Swing migration (preferred for major version upgrades):
# 1. Install new version on a second server in staging mode
# 2. Run full import and sync on the new server
# 3. Compare connector space object counts between old and new
Get-ADSyncConnectorStatistics -ConnectorName 'contoso.com'
Get-ADSyncConnectorStatistics -ConnectorName 'Entra ID Connector'
# 4. If counts match: disable staging on new server, enable staging on old server
# 5. Monitor for 24 hours; decommission old server after verification

# Post-upgrade validation
Get-ADSyncScheduler
Get-ADSyncConnectorRunStatus | Select-Object ConnectorName, Result | Format-Table
Entra Cloud Sync as the Modern Alternative

Microsoft Entra Cloud Sync is a newer, cloud-managed alternative to Entra Connect that uses a lightweight provisioning agent instead of the full sync engine. The agent downloads synchronization rules from the cloud and pushes objects to Entra ID without requiring local SQL, the Synchronization Service Manager, or access to the sync engine's rule editor. Cloud Sync is the recommended choice for new deployments where its feature set meets requirements.
Full sync engine on-premises; SQL required for large deployments
Lightweight agent (~100 MB); config managed from Entra portal
Full Synchronization Rules Editor; arbitrary attribute transforms
Limited to built-in attribute mappings and expression editor
Supported; single Entra tenant from multiple forests
Supported; each forest installs its own agent

# Install and register an Entra Cloud Sync agent
# Download agent from: Entra portal > Hybrid Management > Entra Cloud Sync

# After installing the MSI, register the agent with your tenant
# Open 'Microsoft Azure AD Connect Provisioning Agent Configuration' from Start menu
# Follow wizard: sign in with Hybrid Identity Administrator credentials
# Select Active Directory domain(s) to sync

# Verify agent registration via Graph
Connect-MgGraph -Scopes 'Directory.Read.All'
Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Uri 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/onPremisesPublishingProfiles/provisioning/agents' |
    Select-Object -ExpandProperty value |
    Select-Object machineName, status, appVersion

# Create a Cloud Sync configuration (sync all users from a domain)
$syncConfig = @{
    templateId           = 'AD2AADProvisioning'
    sourceDirectoryName  = 'contoso.com'
    targetDirectoryName  = 'contoso.onmicrosoft.com'
    userMapping          = @{
        sourceDirectoryAttribute = 'userPrincipalName'
        targetDirectoryAttribute = 'userPrincipalName'
    }
}
Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method POST `
    -Uri 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/servicePrincipals/<cloud-sync-sp-id>/synchronization/jobs' `
    -Body ($syncConfig | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 5)

Microsoft Entra Connect is the synchronization engine that makes hybrid identity work — bridging on-premises Active Directory with Microsoft Entra ID. Understanding its three-layer architecture (AD connector, metaverse, Entra connector), authentication method choices, and writeback capabilities is essential for operating a healthy hybrid environment.
Entra Connect syncs every 30 minutes by default; use Start-ADSyncSyncCycle for on-demand sync
PHS is the most resilient auth method — it has no on-premises dependency at sign-in time; PTA requires auth agents to be reachable
Password Hash Sync sends a salted hash of the NTLM hash — never the plaintext or the raw NTLM hash
OU filtering restricts which objects sync; scoping filters on sync rules allow attribute-based exclusion
Custom synchronization rules should use precedence numbers below 100 to avoid conflicting with future default rule updates
Sync errors surface as onPremisesProvisioningErrors on user objects in Graph; fix in AD, then trigger delta sync
Password writeback requires Reset Password, Write lockoutTime, and Write pwdLastSet permissions on the AD connector account
